qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authentication on all SBI API endpoints when OAuth2 is not enforced by NRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is null-byte injection in UDM Nudm_SDM supi path parameters causing Go URL parsing failure and 500 responses. This finding instead concerns AuthorizationCheck returning nil when OAuth2Required is false, which is different vulnerable code and a different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying vulnerability finding identifies the advisory-described null-byte input validation failure in UDM Nudm_SDM supi path handling or the Go URL parsing failure that returns 500 Internal Server Error.
evidence: The advisory GHSA-p9hg-pq3q-v9gv describes URL-encoded null bytes in the Nudm_SubscriberDataManagement supi path parameter reaching URL construction/parsing and producing an invalid control character error with HTTP 500.

The only qualifying finding, missing_sbi_authentication, describes a missing authentication condition in internal/context/context.go where AuthorizationCheck skips token validation when OAuth2Required is false.
Although both involve free5GC UDM SBI endpoints, the reported vulnerable function, guard condition, exploit mechanism, and security consequence differ from the advisory root cause.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 PoC execution is not applicable.
